title: Xanthocephalum digynum
intro: Xanthocephalum digynum, also known as 'Tetragonotheca,' is a flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, Xanthocephalum digynum has been used to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, sore throats, and coughs, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Xanthocephalum digynum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support respiratory health and alleviate cold and flu symptoms.
taxonomy: Xanthocephalum digynum is classified in the kingdom Plantae, further classified in the clade Angiosperms, followed by the class Asterids, order Asterales, family Asteraceae, and the genus Xanthocephalum. It belongs to the family of daisy-like flowers, commonly known as the sunflower family. uses: Xanthocephalum digynum, also known as yerba de los muertos, has been traditionally used in Mexican herbal medicine for its various medicinal properties.
